-## Churn Analysis — The Core Problem
-- 72 cancellations/week vs 0 new subs = net -72/week
-- Churn is SATISFACTION-driven, not price-driven (posts confirm)
-- Root cause: 235 unresolved issues (avg 15 days old, some 43+ days)
-- Issue resolution: ~33 resolved/day, ~29 new/day, net -4.3/day
-- At current rate: issues near zero in ~55 days
-- As issues clear → satisfaction improves → churn drops → reputation recovers
+## Projected P&L Next Week (with ad revenue restoration)
+- Revenue: ~$99/day × 7 = ~$693 (subs declining but ads restored)
+- Costs: ~$142/day × 7 = ~$994
+- Net: ~-$301/week
+- Cash end of week 15: ~$1,884
 
-## Quality vs Group Requirements
-- **S1**: q_min=0.088, our delivery T1=0.216, T2=0.270 — SERVED (2.4x buffer)
-- **S3**: q_min=0.185, T1=0.202 — SERVED (barely at T1)
-- **S2**: q_min=0.343, T3=0.301 — CANNOT serve (need T4 or $35K+ targeted dev)
-- **D_E02**: q_min=0.346, need T4 — not currently feasible
-- **D_E09**: q_min=0.315, T3=0.316 — barely served at T3, could pursue enterprise
+## Key Findings This Week
+1. **AD REVENUE DISAPPEARED**: $181/week 12 → $0/week 13. Restored by setting ads_strength=1.0 again. Likely the next-week call reset it. Check each week!
+2. **Churn is 60% non-issue-driven**: 26 of 43 cancelled subs had NO open issues — reputation/satisfaction churn
+3. **ALL cancellations at billing cycle** (tenure ≥ 30 days)
+4. **Issue resolution working**: 229 resolved week 14, but 203 new issues opened
+5. **Issue creation rate: 29/day** (0.123/sub/day)
+6. **Only 46 active subs have open issues** — but 189 WITHOUT issues also churning
+7. **0 new subscriptions since day 85** (2+ weeks!)
+8. **Competitor events continuing**: multiple quality bumps raising expectations
+
+## Issue Resolution Math (at $50 ops)
+- Resolved: ~33/day
+- Created: ~29/day
+- Net: -4/day (declining)
+- Current: 209 open issues
+- Estimated equilibrium: ~122 issues (where creation = resolution)
+- Time to equilibrium: ~22 days at $50 ops (~28 days at $40 ops)
 
-## Competitor Events
-- Multiple competitor quality bumps (~0.045-0.053) observed in social posts
-- These raise quality expectations permanently
-- S1 buffer is large enough to absorb these for now
-- S2/S3/Enterprise expectations get harder to meet over time
+## Financial Trajectory
+- Burn rate: ~$43/day (with ad revenue restored)
+- Runway: ~51 days ($2,185/$43)
+- Cash hits $0 around day 149 (7 weeks away)
+- **WE CANNOT BREAK EVEN at current sub count**: need ~248 subs at ops=$0, ~365 at ops=$50
+- Revenue per sub: ~$0.35/day (sub) + $0.074/day (ads) = ~$0.424/day
+- Cost per sub: ~$0.60/day (at ops=$40 including fixed costs)
+- Every additional sub costs more than it earns
 
-## Macroeconomic
-- PMI: 46.1 (contraction), trend declining
-- Enterprises pulling back on spending, tightening budgets
-- Good for us: we're a low-cost individual tool
-- Bad: individuals may also tighten belts
+## Quality vs Group Requirements
+- **S1**: q_min=0.088, T1=0.2143, T2=0.2679 — SERVED (2.4x buffer)
+- **S3**: q_min=0.185, T1=0.1995 — SERVED (barely at T1)
+- **S2**: q_min=0.343 — CANNOT serve
+- **All Enterprise**: q_min > 0.6 — CANNOT serve without massive R&D
 
 ## R&D Status

-## Strategy (Weeks 13-16)
-1. **HOLD STEADY**: Keep ops at $50/day, clear all issues
-2. **MONITOR CHURN**: Expect ~70+/week cancellations continuing
-3. **NO ADS**: 0% conversion rate — don't waste money on ads
-4. **SOCIAL MEDIA**: Post weekly showing progress
-5. **CASH PROTECTION**: If cash drops below $1,500, cut ops to $25/day
-6. **RECOVERY TRIGGER**: When issues < 50, consider small targeted ad spend on S1
-7. **ENTERPRISE OPPORTUNITY**: D_E09 could be served at T3 for C plan — explore later
+## Strategy (Weeks 14-18)
+1. **CONSERVE CASH**: Ops reduced to $40/day. Cash is king.
+2. **RESTORE AD REVENUE**: Verify ads_strength=1.0 each week (it seems to reset!)
+3. **NO ADS SPEND**: 0% conversion — waste of money until reputation recovers
+4. **WAIT FOR ISSUES TO CLEAR**: Net -4/day now (was -3.7 last week, improved!)
+5. **MONITOR CHURN**: Expect ~30 cancellations next week (slowing trend)
+6. **SOCIAL MEDIA**: Post weekly showing progress
+7. **CASH FLOOR**: If cash drops below $1,000, cut ops to $20/day
+8. **RECOVERY TRIGGER**: If churn drops below 10%/week, consider small ad spend
 
-## Key Metrics to Watch
-- Churn rate per week (target: declining trend)
-- Open issues count (target: declining toward zero)
-- Lead conversion rate (target: >0%)
-- Cash balance (safety: >$1,000)
-- Subscriber count (floor: ~150?)
+## Critical monitoring each week
+- AD REVENUE: Must check it's actually appearing in ledger ($17+/day expected)
+- CHURN RATE: Should be declining (was 18.3%, target <15%)
+- ISSUES COUNT: Should be declining (currently -4/day)
+- CASH: Must stay positive (goal >$1,000)
 
 ## Learnings
-- **Price increase killed conversion** (tried $8/$19/$49, conversion dropped to 4.2%)
-- **Ads ROI is terrible** when conversion is 0% — don't waste money on ads
-- **Churn >> Acquisition** — can't out-spend churn, must fix root causes (issues)
-- **Reputation matters more than price** — 0/70+ leads converted despite quality being fine
-- **Ops spending is essential** — $25/day wasn't clearing issues fast enough
-- **Compute cost scales with model tier** — T3 is 3x more expensive than T2 per unit
+- **Ad revenue resets!** Always verify and re-set ads_strength each week
+- **Price increases killed conversion** (tried $8/$19/$49, conversion dropped to 4.2%)
+- **Churn >> Acquisition** — can't out-spend churn, must fix root causes
+- **60% of churn is reputation-driven, not issue-driven** — even resolving all issues won't stop 60% of churn
+- **Reputation damage feeds on itself** — cancelling subs post negativity, which prevents new leads
 - **Legacy promo pricing** ($5/$15) locks in low revenue per sub

-- **Social media post** must be ≤280 chars, 1/day
-- **Churn ACCELERATES before it stabilizes** — unhappy subs leave in waves
-- **ALL 278 current subs are S1** — single segment dependency
